mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate recession risk repricing and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is already above 6%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and durable goods demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in quarterly return regressions, or positive returns fail to materialize when HOUST declines despite rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes where INDPRO rises but input costs outpace output prices, causing Rank IC to turn negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week as markets price in improved economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 revisions, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of high-GDP-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 4.0% annualized threshold, inflation expectations force markets to anticipate aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of high-inflation periods, or the threshold fails to predict returns when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks rather than demand pull.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer income and corporate earnings expectations to drive positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Interaction effect vanishes when wage growth outpaces productivity, or negative returns dominate when PAYEMS rises but UNRATE stagnates above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, immediately pressuring corporate revenue forecasts and triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during early-cycle phases where retail sales dips are offset by inventory drawdowns, or positive returns exceed 60% of triggered instances.
